one_line_definition: A single transducer emits a sound pulse and receives the reflected echo; ToF to the reflecting surface gives thickness

aliases_seen: transit time, time delay, tB, tP
one_line_definition: The elapsed time for an ultrasonic pulse to travel from transducer to reflecting surface and back; used to calculate thickness via d = vt/2
Measures sub-nanosecond changes in ToF by tracking the CW frequency shift needed to maintain fixed echo phase, achieving theoretical wear resolution of 0.015 µm
Correction applied to ultrasonic wear measurements to remove the apparent thickness change caused by thermal expansion and acoustic velocity variation with temperature
A device that converts electrical pulses to mechanical (ultrasonic) waves and vice versa, used to both generate and receive ultrasound
The height of the worn flat area on a cutting tool flank, used as the standard ISO metric for tool wear; uniform-wear limit VB = 0.3 mm
A transducer coupled to a buffer rod (delay line) that separates the electrical excitation pulse from the material echoes in time, enabling near-surface measurements

aliases_seen: area under waveform, echo energy metric
one_line_definition: The integral of absolute amplitude of the A-scan waveform over a gated region; increases with flank/nose wear as flat reflecting area grows
A fixed internal reflector (notch or hole) placed at a known depth in the specimen that shifts with temperature alongside the wear-face echo, allowing thermal effects to be subtracted

A frequency-domain technique in which subsurface holes near the wear surface create constructive/destructive interference dips in the FFT spectrum; dip positions track remaining material thickness independently of global temperature

aliases_seen: buffer rod, non-clad buffer rod, clad buffer rod
one_line_definition: A steel waveguide rod that mechanically separates a room-temperature transducer from a hot barrel surface, enabling ultrasonic coupling at elevated process temperatures
High-temperature ultrasonic transducer formed by spraying bismuth-titanate / PZT gel directly onto a hot surface and poling; operates up to 600 °C, no couplant required
The distance between the inner barrel wall and the screw flight tip in an extruder; a key process health indicator combining barrel wear, screw wear, and misalignment
Short-timescale positional variation of the screw shaft from its mean position during extrusion, caused by asymmetric melt loads; distinguishable from steady-state misalignment
Steady-state deviation of the screw shaft axis from the extruder bore axis, determined by time-averaging clearance measurements
Two separate transducers (transmitter and receiver) set at an inclination angle to generate refracted waves at a desired mode and angle in the inspection target
The incidence angle at which the refracted longitudinal wave in a medium reaches 90°; beyond this angle only transverse waves exist in the second medium
Reduction of wall thickness in the Zircaloy-2 calandria tubes of a CANDU reactor caused by contact with LISS nozzles; minimum acceptable remaining thickness 0.6 mm
A 2-D cross-sectional ultrasonic image formed by scanning the transducer and plotting echo time (depth) against position, used here to map CT wall thickness along the tube axis
Classical wear quantification by weighing a specimen before and after a test; limited by inability to distinguish material removal from material transfer
Ratio of useful echo amplitude to surrounding noise amplitude; governs time-delay measurement accuracy in ultrasonic thickness gauging
Difference in acoustic impedance (ρv) between two materials at an interface; determines the fraction of ultrasonic energy reflected vs. transmitted
